title: Senator Dave Cortese (D-San Jose) has introduced Senate Bill (SB) 9 to give vulnerable youth the ability to stay in the extended foster care system until the age of 26.

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 9 will extend the age of jurisdiction for voluntary extended foster care from age 21 to age 26.",
    "Youth coming out of both the foster care and juvenile justice systems are eligible to receive extended jurisdiction under this proposal.",
    "Extending foster care beyond age 21 is expected to have positive results for vulnerable young persons.",
    "There is extensive data supporting positive outcomes from extending foster care from ages 18 to 21.",
    "Researchers agree that extending foster care beyond age 21 will yield similar positive results for those under court supervision.",
    "Many foster youth lack the stability and support available from a family.",
    "The extension of benefits may aid foster youth in stabilizing their educational and employment situations."
  ]
}
```
